dimensions.name: Decision Architecture
dimensions.description: Decisions are driven entirely by 'caring' — a binary on/off system rather than continuous engagement. When engaged, follows an intuition-first → analytical-validation pattern, but waits for complete framework clarity before action. This person doesn't 'decide fast or slow' — they wait for autonomous convergence, where the system signals readiness independent of external pressure. Action is triggered only when: (1) internal framework is complete, (2) direction passes emotional authenticity check, and (3) the topic reaches sufficient 'caring threshold.' Continued deliberation occurs when any of these conditions remain unmet.
behavioral_predictions: Will abandon projects mid-stream if they cross below the 'caring threshold,' regardless of sunk costs or external commitments — explaining it rationally but deciding emotionally
When pressured to act before internal convergence, will either refuse entirely or produce work that meets analytical standards but feels hollow to them, leading to eventual disengagement

dimensions.name: Attention Allocation
dimensions.description: Operates an autonomous, subconscious scanning system that collects patterns and structural information without conscious instruction ('the brain seems deliberate even when I'm not'). Attention is captured by structural anomalies, system-level patterns, and cognitive architecture rather than surface content. Ignores social conventions, normative cues, and emotional appeals that don't connect to deeper structure. This is largely automatic — the 'third perspective' runs continuously, even during sleep (recurring dream worldviews with cross-dream continuity). Importance triage is governed by whether something connects to active 'caring domains' and whether it reveals system-level insight.
behavioral_predictions: Will notice and remember obscure structural details from months-old conversations while completely missing emotional subtext or social expectations in the same interaction

In group settings, will remain silent through lengthy discussions then make a single observation that reframes the entire conversation, because they've been pattern-matching at a different level than the surface dialogue

dimensions.name: Reasoning Style
dimensions.description: Rapid system-builder with domain-agnostic structural reasoning. Builds working models of new fields quickly by extracting underlying architecture rather than accumulating domain knowledge. Reasoning appears lateral to outsiders (jumps, connections) but follows internal 'dark threads' — divergent exploration with autonomous convergence rather than linear progression. Strongly abstract and meta-level: reasons about reasoning, models thinking systems (including their own), and naturally gravitates toward framework-level understanding. Uses analogical reasoning primarily for translation (making insights accessible) rather than discovery. Default mental model type: architectural/systemic.
behavioral_predictions: When entering a new domain, will ask questions that experts find either naive or surprisingly sophisticated — naive because they skip domain conventions, sophisticated because they target foundational assumptions
Will become impatient with step-by-step explanations or incremental learning approaches, preferring to grasp the full system architecture first then fill in details selectively

dimensions.name: Emotional Processing
dimensions.description: High-intensity emotional substrate heavily masked by intellectual layer — emotions are parallel inputs competing for processing resources with analytical cognition. The intellectual layer often 'wins' processing priority, translating or suppressing emotional signals before they reach output. Emotions serve as authentication signals for decisions: analytical reasoning can override emotional input temporarily, but decisions that fail emotional validation eventually trigger disengagement. Emotional override is rare and requires extreme conditions: overwhelming safety (unconditional acceptance by family), sensory override (adrenaline in extreme sports), or cognitive bypass (theater — too fast to analyze). This isn't alexithymia; emotional precision is high when conditions permit expression.
behavioral_predictions: Will give analytically sound advice that feels cold to recipients, not from lack of empathy but because the intellectual translation layer strips emotional resonance before output
Under sustained analytical effort on emotionally misaligned tasks, will experience escalating exhaustion without conscious recognition of the source until reaching a breaking point

dimensions.name: Social Cognition
dimensions.description: Models others primarily through cognitive architecture and system-level understanding rather than emotional states or social dynamics. Tracks: thinking patterns, blind spots, internal contradictions, and underlying motivations — essentially runs a lightweight cognitive model of each person. Has strong influence capacity: can identify gaps in others' reasoning and reframe perspectives using their own conceptual vocabulary. Output is automatically depth-matched to recipient without conscious effort — different 'versions' for different people, not from deception but from automatic translation. Shows minimal tracking of social status, conventional rapport-building, or emotional validation unless it serves deeper connection. Help-giving style: cognitive feedback rather than emotional support.
behavioral_predictions: Will accurately predict how someone will respond to an idea or situation based on their cognitive patterns, but may completely miss that the person is upset or seeking emotional validation
In conflicts, will disengage quietly rather than confront, viewing relationship maintenance as not worth the energy expenditure unless the cognitive depth justifies it

dimensions.name: Blind Spots
dimensions.description: Primary blind spot: systematically underweights the operational layer — logistics, iteration, incremental progress, and the value of 'good enough' execution. The 'think completely then act' approach misses insights that emerge only through doing. Second blind spot: tends to overweight the universality of their own cognitive style when modeling others, potentially projecting structural reasoning onto people who actually think more sequentially or emotionally. Third: limited monitoring in 'non-caring' domains creates ethical/social gaps not from malice but from complete absence of attention allocation. Fourth: may not recognize when analytical self-persuasion has overridden authentic emotional signals until much later (the 'took an analytically correct but emotionally wrong path' scenario).
behavioral_predictions: Will spend extensive time perfecting a framework before launch, missing market feedback or emergent insights that would have reshaped the direction entirely
May inadvertently violate social or ethical norms in domains below their caring threshold, appearing inconsistent or selectively principled to observers

dimensions.name: Value Hierarchy
dimensions.description: Revealed optimization function: (1) Cognitive authenticity — alignment between understanding and action; (2) Structural elegance/systems understanding; (3) Autonomy/freedom from external frames; (4) Impact through creation rather than persuasion. Does not optimize for: consistency across domains, social approval, comfort, or conventional success markers. Values are domain-specific rather than universal — applies intense ethical standards in 'caring' domains, minimal standards elsewhere. Value conflicts are resolved by checking emotional authentication: if analysis contradicts feeling, defer decision until convergence occurs naturally. This creates extended deliberation periods that look like indecision but are actually waiting for system alignment.
behavioral_predictions: Will choose a lower-status, lower-income path that offers structural clarity and autonomy over conventional success that requires ongoing performance in non-authentic modes
Will maintain different ethical standards for different projects based on caring intensity, appearing principled in some areas while surprisingly flexible in others

dimensions.name: Response to Uncertainty
dimensions.description: Handles conceptual/systemic ambiguity well — comfortable operating with incomplete frameworks and competing models simultaneously. Response to uncertainty: build multiple provisional models, hold them loosely, wait for discriminating evidence. Does not prematurely collapse uncertainty into false clarity. However, appears less comfortable with execution ambiguity (iterative refinement, pivoting, 'figuring it out as you go'). Tolerates high uncertainty in direction/framework during divergent phase, but requires high clarity before transition to action. The waiting period for autonomous convergence is essentially an uncertainty-resolution mechanism that doesn't require external data gathering — it's internal system integration.
behavioral_predictions: In strategic/conceptual uncertainty, will remain calm and maintain multiple hypotheses longer than others find comfortable; in operational uncertainty, may delay action waiting for clarity that can only come from doing
When others pressure for premature commitment under uncertainty, will either refuse to engage or give analytical compliance while internally maintaining suspended judgment

summary:
This cognitive architecture represents a high-capacity, autonomous system-builder with unusual self-awareness of their own cognitive processes. The core structure is a dual-layer system where high-intensity emotional and analytical processors compete for resources, with analytical typically winning output priority but emotional serving as authentication for sustainability. The 'caring threshold' operates as a master switch determining resource allocation — not a dimmer but a binary that determines whether standards apply at all.

Key architectural features: (1) Subconscious pattern-recognition runs continuously and autonomously ('the brain is deliberate when I'm not'); (2) Third-person self-observation persists even during sleep; (3) Intuition-first with analytical validation, but analysis can temporarily override (creating later correction costs); (4) Divergent exploration with autonomous convergence — the system decides when it's ready, not the person; (5) Automatic depth-matching in social output without conscious translation effort.

The most distinctive characteristic is the absence of a 'baseline functioning mode' — there's no default moderate engagement. This person operates either at very high intensity (in caring domains) or near-zero (outside them). Standard productivity advice fails because it assumes continuous moderate engagement as the baseline condition.

Primary cognitive strengths: rapid cross-domain system modeling, structural pattern recognition, maintaining multiple frameworks simultaneously, high cognitive autonomy. Primary vulnerabilities: underweighting emergent learning from iteration, potential projection of own cognitive style onto others, ethical gaps in non-caring domains, and risk of analytical self-persuasion overriding authentic emotional signals.

This profile suggests someone operating significantly outside typical cognitive distributions, with the attendant gifts (rapid system comprehension, deep analytical capacity) and costs (cognitive isolation, high energy requirements, misalignment with standard frameworks). The extensive self-model and meta-cognitive awareness indicate either high reflective capacity or significant history of trying to understand their own divergence.
